items.front: When is coordination required for parachute jumps?
items.back: Coordinate pertinent information before and at the end of each jump or series of jumps that begins or ends in your area of jurisdiction.

items.front: How are parachute operations handled in Class A, B, and C airspace?
items.back: Authorize them only within the designated jump airspace, separate nonparticipating aircraft from that airspace, and impose any needed conditions or restrictions.

items.front: How are parachute jump requests handled in Class D airspace with a functioning tower?
items.back: Authorize the jump with respect to known or observed traffic and issue advisory information as needed for safe conduct of the operation.

items.front: What advisories are given to known nonparticipating aircraft for parachute operations in other control airspace?
items.back: Issue the jump location, time, duration, and the altitude from which the jump will be made.
